segments.text: Thank you.
Thank you.

Tonight's guest was featured on last Monday night's show.
It took so long to share all of his experiences, and he's had quite a few,
that we didn't have enough time to go over the questions I wanted to ask him.
So I asked him if he'd be interested in coming back for a part two
where I could do a Q&A with him, and luckily he agreed to do that.
So he's back to answer all of my questions.
Of course, I'm talking about Brian.
Brian, thanks so much for coming back.
Absolutely, Vic, no problem.
That's really appreciated.
And Brian, for anyone who missed last Monday night's show,
please tell us about your experiences and kind of a flyover,
a Cliff Notes version of those experiences.
well it happened in december of 2005 sometime after midnight when i heard my dog
acting strangely outside and since this is a clip notes version the howling that i heard that caused
him to act the way he was was something that i'd never heard before in my first uh first episode
with you, I commented on how it sounded like a renaissance howl, like a dog was trapped in a
barrel. So I wanted to go investigate. I like animals and I thought maybe I could help this
dog out if it was trapped. So I took a spotlight and a 45 with me just in case, you know, something
was in case the dog was beyond help, something was wrong. I walked about 300 yards or so to the
north when I realized that the howling that I was hearing was not coming from the culvert that the
towns were putting in, the town was putting in. It was coming from the woods back behind the old
mayor's house. So I looked at the .45 in my hand and I went home and grabbed a bigger rifle,
something with night vision, a Gen 1 night vision, went back, crossed the, went down a hill,
As I said, it was about 300, 350 yards from the house.
I went down a hill, crossed a road, crossed a ditch line, walked back.
And as I was shining the spotlight, this thing started tearing through the woods right towards me.
Had no idea.
I couldn't see anything because the woods are still thick.
And I dropped the spotlight and slammed the bolt shut.
The firearm I was using was an HK-91.
The bolt's on the side, the bolt release.
And when I slammed that thing shut, they're pretty stout and they make a lot of noise.
Whatever was running at me that I didn't know what was running at me, it stopped when it heard that sound.
I bent down and I picked up the spotlight.
And I started shining across the area where I thought I could see what was coming through the woods.
And having hunted before and been outside many times at night over the years, I was looking about four feet off the ground.

I swept from left to right and then went far enough right that I knew okay what I was hearing wasn there So I started coming back to the left and I shined up for some reason I thought okay I look up a tree I didn think it was a raccoon but hey I look
I shined, and I shined up a little higher, and that's when I hit it.
I hit this thing that had, for the better way of putting it, they were red eyes.
and I'm looking at something that shouldn't exist.
I'm looking at this,
at the time I didn't know how else to put it.
Now I know to call it a dog man.
It's standing there.
It's got its hands pushing down on this limb.
And I say hands, not paws.
And it's looking at me.
I could see it breathing.
I could see its chest heaving.
It's breathing out, and you could see the steam coming out of its mouth.
And it's staring at me, and I'm staring at it.
And it felt like it lasted forever, but in reality, it was probably maybe 15, 20 seconds.
And then it turned and ran, and it moved so fast.
It ran, like I said, probably between 30 and 40 yards flat ground through the woods.
Then uphill for another, I don't know, a bit until it hit the main road.
I could hear the gravel that was loose that had been put in up there just kind of jar as it hit it.
I made my way home, spent the rest of the night trying to determine what it is I heard and had no idea.
I talked to you a little bit about how about a month later that there were tracks in the snow
we had a snowstorm and my neighbor I asked him to come over he's a bit older than me he'd never seen
tracks like that I had met and talked with a man from the ODNR he was a new guy out I belong to
local gun club so he came out and did some talk there and I got his card and I called that number
He happened to be in the next town over, and he came out, and he took some pictures, and he said he'd never seen anything like that.
He had no idea what they could be from.
They looked like a dog's paw, but he elongated much longer.
So after everybody left, I turned around and looked while they were all standing there.
We're looking at these tracks, and they were the ones that I initially saw were standing like shoulder-width apart in the snow by the house.
by the sidewalk going up to the house and then as I looked you could see these
tracks and they were they were eight feet apart by pedal it looked like it
hopped in the snow like one here and then hop to the next one and hop to the
next one till I hit the railroad tracks so after the guys left I went in the
house and grabbed my rifle again and my son he wanted to go with me and he's 12
at the time he he later became a marine so i'm pretty proud of him anyway we uh
hoofed it down the railroad tracks following this track or these tracks um now when it snows
and trains pass over the snow there's a buildup of snow between the two sets of railroad tracks
but in the track itself where the railroad ties are the snow gets blown each side to the rail
but I could see a track here and a track there because it was in the snow and the
in the gravel or sometimes I'd catch it just as it stepped on the snow on the
tie and we walked for about a mile we I tracked it and then just lost it and I
don't know if it just popped off the tracks into the woods that would be my
best guess but again being able to hop so far I didn't even know where to start
looking we went back home at the time the howling that we heard because my my then wife at the time
had stepped out on the back porch on the deck and she heard it the best way to describe that how
at the time was the um the how from the american war within london when it's on the moors it had
that kind of renaissance sound.
And now I found something that is almost identical.
And that was the sound that was heard up in Canada.
Somebody was outside their home.

You see a car off to the right and there was woods and stuff to the front of them And to my knowledge I checked back there a few times no one been able to identify this thing And that what it sounded like
So we've had other encounters.
My youngest son, about a year ago, he and I went fishing at the pond at the gun club.
And usually I take, whenever I go outside, I carry a gun.
This time I didn't.

Did you hear it?

it was just for a moment it sounded guttural but i really wasn't i was trying not to catch the line
i was reeling in and i was fishing off the bottom i really didn't want to catch the line that runs
from the the windmill i said i said nobody i didn't see it and his eyes were just huge and it

you know really what did it look like you know where was it at when you first saw it what where

i went over with him that multiple times and the story never changed

that I saw it, as much time as I loved being outdoors and spending time in the woods, hiking

on everything and there's nothing else i can say but it's uh it's something that shouldn't exist

now what what else what else could exist? So yeah, that's

I think that whole idea that whole question about what else

that makes an experience like that so hard to deal with. Can

the first one.

and holding this in the back of my mind all this time,
I often wondered if just perhaps, perhaps these things are,
they can pick up on, you know, maybe death.

having this near-death experience, maybe that's what drew it to me.
This is something that I've thought about many times.
and it's I have no
no way to go from there other than saying perhaps
you know that's something that
may be possible I don't know

that was just his thing and that's what woke me up was he was snapping the end of his chain

they continue to move.
